steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Command of Evidence (Quantitative) question. The passage claims that "most tribal nations in California are less than 50 square miles in total area" and uses the Los Coyotes Band as a supporting example. The blank must be filled with the actual area of the Los Coyotes Band from the table — a value under 50 that directly supports the claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '13.50 square miles': 13.50 is the area of the La Jolla Band, not the Los Coyotes Band. This is the wrong tribe's data. ✗
  • Choice B — '53.68 square miles': 53.68 belongs to the Agua Caliente Band and is also above 50 square miles, which would contradict the passage's claim rather than support it. ✗
  • Choice C — '9.36 square miles': 9.36 is the area of the Pauma Band — again, the wrong tribe. ✗
  • Choice D — '39.21 square miles': The table shows the Los Coyotes Band of Cahuilla and Cupeño Indians at 39.21 square miles. This is under 50, correctly attributed to the named tribe, and directly supports the passage's claim. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
